message:
fix(win32): fix include order for mingw-w64 cross-compilation

Include winsock2.h before windows.h, then bcrypt.h (which needs
Windows types). Previous order had bcrypt.h first, causing
"unknown type name 'ULONG'" errors with mingw-w64.

Verified: make build-win32 succeeds with x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc 13.
Closes bd-hsxb.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "bd-h63b", "title": "Reconcile README test counts after latest tree growth", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "docs", "created_at": "2026-03-28T05:09:03.606017605Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-28T05:10:02.487765029Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-28T05:10:02.487443100Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id":"bd-howq","title":"Fix test_profile_compat to work under all profiles, not just CORE","description":"## Problem
tests/unit/runtime/test_profile_compat.c line 26-30 has:
```c
TEST(profile_active_is_core) {
    asx_profile_id id = asx_profile_active();
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_CORE);
}
```

This test FAILS under any non-CORE profile (POSIX, WIN32, etc.) because it hardcodes the expected profile as CORE. When running `make test PROFILE=POSIX`, this test causes a spurious failure.

## What to fix
Replace the hardcoded assertion with one that checks the COMPILED profile:
```c
TEST(profile_active_matches_compiled) {
    asx_profile_id id = asx_profile_active();
#if defined(ASX_PROFILE_POSIX)
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_POSIX);
#elif defined(ASX_PROFILE_WIN32)
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_WIN32);
#elif defined(ASX_PROFILE_FREESTANDING)
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_FREESTANDING);
/* ... etc for each profile ... */
#else
    ASSERT_EQ((int)id, (int)ASX_PROFILE_ID_CORE);
#endif
}
```

Also rename the test function and its RUN_TEST call from `profile_active_is_core` to `profile_active_matches_compiled`.

## Files
- tests/unit/runtime/test_profile_compat.c (modify)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:14:27.893180461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T16:15:18.689295597Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T16:15:18.689017440Z","close_reason":"Fixed profile_active_is_core test to profile_active_matches_compiled with #ifdef guards for all 9 profiles. Test now passes under CORE (default) and POSIX profiles. Verified: make test passes (CORE), make test PROFILE=POSIX passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-hsxb","title":"Win32: install mingw-w64 and verify cross-compilation","description":"## Background
The Win32 platform adapter (src/platform/win32/hooks.c) was implemented in the current session with all hooks: QPC clock, BCryptGenRandom entropy, WSAPoll/SleepEx reactor, CreateThread blocking pool, stderr+OutputDebugString log, and asx_win32_hooks_install() export function. The header include/asx/platform/win32.h was created. The Makefile build-win32 target was added.

However, this code has NEVER been compiled because mingw-w64 is not installed on this system. The code compiles correctly on CORE and POSIX profiles (since it's behind #ifdef ASX_PROFILE_WIN32).

## What to do
1. Install mingw-w64: sudo apt install gcc-mingw-w64-x86-64
2. Run: make build-win32
3. Fix any compilation errors
4. Verify the CI workflow handles Win32 cross-compilation

## Why this matters
The Win32 adapter is complete but UNPROVEN. Cross-compilation is the minimum bar for a 'working' Win32 adapter since we can't run Windows binaries on this Linux host.

## Files
- src/platform/win32/hooks.c (fix if needed)
- include/asx/platform/win32.h (fix if needed)
- Makefile (fix build-win32 target if needed)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:50.147262930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:13:50.147262930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

The header include/asx/platform/win32.h was created. The Makefile build-win32 target was added.

However, this code has NEVER been compiled because mingw-w64 is not installed on this system. The code compiles correctly on CORE and POSIX profiles (since it's behind #ifdef ASX_PROFILE_WIN32).

 {"id": "bd-hwb", "title": "EPIC: Core Semantics and Guarantee Substitution in ANSI C/C99", "description": "Background:
Rust compile-time guarantees are absent in C. We must reconstitute equivalent runtime/proof guarantees via explicit mechanisms.

Goal:
Implement asx_core semantic primitives and safety scaffolding with deterministic failure behavior.

Core obligations:
- explicit state authorities
- generation-safe handles and ownership boundaries
- ghost monitors and linearity checks
- strict resource exhaustion semantics
- canonical error taxonomy and stability policy

Why this serves users:
- correctness under pressure for embedded systems
- predictable failure semantics for high-stakes systems
- confidence that performance tuning does not erase guarantees.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:47:42.038789705Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T19:59:25.155834562Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T19:59:25.155810537Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["core", "phase-3", "phase-4", "safety", "semantics"]}
 {"id": "bd-hwb.1", "title": "Implement core API types: status, errors, outcomes, budgets, cancellation, and IDs", "description": "Background:
These primitives are shared across all runtime behavior and therefore must be stable, deterministic, and audit-friendly.

Subtasks:
- Define status/error taxonomy with stable code families.
- Define outcome types and ordering semantics.
- Define budget and cancellation core types with explicit failure semantics.
- Define ID formats and generation strategy for handle safety.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Types are fully documented and covered by unit tests.
- Error/status semantics are deterministic and profile-agnostic.
- Public API naming and visibility are consistent.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:49:09.812710740Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T05:10:04.652897529Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T05:10:04.652875979Z", "close_reason": "done. Evidence: tests/unit/core/test_cancel.c. Additional evidence: tests/e2e/e2e_core_lifecycle.c, tests/e2e/e2e_foundational_contracts.c, tests/invariant/lifecycle/test_lifecycle_legality.c; fixtures/rust_reference/, build/conformance/, .beads/issues.jsonl", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["api", "core", "phase-3", "safety"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-hwb.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-296.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-hwb.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-296.29", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-hwb.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-hwb", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-hwb.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-ix8.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-hwb.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-ix8.8", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-hwb.10", "title": "Implement protothread-style task poll contract and region-arena captured-state safety", "description": "Background:
Plan sections 6.8.A and 7.4 require a safe C replacement for async/await that preserves lifecycle semantics without stack-lifetime footguns.

Subtasks:
- Implement coroutine-style task macros (`ASX_CO_BEGIN`, `ASX_CO_YIELD`, `ASX_CO_END`) with explicit resume state.
- Enforce region-arena ownership of captured task state; no malloc-owned suspended state in kernel profiles.
- Add explicit yield-safety lint gate that flags non-const locals/raw internal pointers persisting across yield unless waived.
- Define spawn contract for state sizing/finalization so suspended tasks cannot leak on region close.
- Add tests for suspend/resume ordering, cancellation checkpoints, and region-close cleanup.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Poll contract is deterministic and fixture-tested.
- No leaked task state on cancellation or region closure.
- Yield-safety violations are caught automatically by CI lint/static checks.
- Macro workflow is documented with explicit do/dont examples.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:56:45.639588066Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-27T06:08:14.924460519Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-27T06:08:14.924440612Z", "close_reason": "done. Unit test evidence: tests/unit/core/test_protothread.c, tests/unit/core/test_safety_posture.c", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["core", "coroutines", "hard-part"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-hwb.10", "depends_on_id": "bd-hwb", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-hwb.10", "depends_on_id": "bd-hwb.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-hwb.10", "depends_on_id": "bd-hwb.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

@@ -22,13 +22,14 @@
 #include <stdio.h>
 #include <string.h>
 
-/* winsock2.h must precede windows.h, while bcrypt.h needs Windows types. */
+/* Include order: winsock2.h before windows.h (avoids redefinition warnings
+ * on MSVC), then bcrypt.h which requires Windows types from windows.h. */
 #ifndef WIN32_LEAN_AND_MEAN
 #define WIN32_LEAN_AND_MEAN
 #endif
-#include <bcrypt.h>
-#include <windows.h>
 #include <winsock2.h>
+#include <windows.h>
+#include <bcrypt.h>
 
 #define ASX_WIN32_NSEC_PER_SEC 1000000000ULL
 #define ASX_WIN32_FILETIME_UNIX_EPOCH_100NS 116444736000000000ULL
